hero.title: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu Wiki
hero.description: Enter a cursed jungle with up to four explorers, complete contracts, recover forbidden treasures and survive monsters that distort reality and turn your own senses against you.

gameFeature.title: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Lead up to four explorers into a cursed jungle, complete the captain's contracts, recover forbidden treasure and survive a spreading madness that distorts everything you see and hear.

tools.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu
tools.subtitle: Everything you need to survive the cursed jungle, from your first expedition to the deepest extraction.

releaseDatePlatforms.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Release Date, Platforms and Editions
releaseDatePlatforms.intro: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu launched on July 15, 2026 for PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. Compare the Standard Edition, the Deluxe Edition and the launch bonuses before you buy.

items.details: Both editions launched on the same date.
Regional prices vary by country and storefront.
The discount was unlocked after the game passed 800,000 Steam wishlists.
Includes the complete expedition, contract, treasure, combat and madness systems.
Adds two exclusive playable explorer characters for wider cosmetic choice.
Adds Cthulhu-themed appearances for the knife, oil lamp, flintlock musket and matchlock musket.

items.standard: Not included in the core console Standard Edition package
items.details: Adds a new contract set in a unique area of the cursed jungle. On Steam it was also offered as a separate free claim, and only the host needs access for the whole party to play.
Contains the official original music from the game.
Adds two ornate sword appearances. Steam players must claim the pack during the first two weeks after launch.

items.standard: Steam, Epic Games Store and Xbox PC Store
items.deluxe: Steam, Epic Games Store and Xbox PC Store
items.details: The Windows version requires a 64-bit operating system.
No PlayStation 4, Xbox One or Nintendo Switch version is listed.

items.standard: Xbox Play Anywhere, cross-platform co-op and cloud support
items.deluxe: Xbox Play Anywhere, cross-platform co-op and cloud support
items.details: The Xbox listing supports single-player and online co-op for two to four players.

beginnerGuide.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Learn how to prepare aboard the galleon, divide limited equipment, escort the ox cart and complete the captain's contract. Careful movement and an early retreat are usually safer than fighting everything you meet.

steps.title: Choose a Contract on the Galleon
steps.description: Speak with the captain and select an expedition contract. Contracts can require specific discoveries, rescued survivors or a minimum total treasure value.
priorities: Read the required loot value before leaving.
Choose a destination that fits the crew's experience.
Agree on the conditions that will trigger an early retreat.

steps.title: Divide Weapons and Tools
steps.description: The captain supplies shared weapons, equipment and resources rather than a complete personal set for every explorer. Assign quiet combat, firearm, lighting and treasure-finding duties before departure.
priorities: Give the bow or another quiet option to a confident ranged player.
Keep knives available as rain-proof emergency weapons.
Do not place every firearm or lamp on the same explorer.

steps.title: Protect and Use the Ox Cart
steps.description: The ox cart follows the expedition and stores collected treasure, supplies and spare equipment. Deposit valuables regularly instead of carrying everything while exploring dangerous ruins.
priorities: Keep the route around the cart clear.
Return valuable items to the cart before entering another dangerous area.
Listen carefully before responding to the cart's horn, because madness can imitate familiar sounds.

steps.title: Control Noise in the Jungle
steps.description: Running, cutting through vegetation, firing guns, crossing water and other loud sounds can wake the jungle and attract additional threats. Slow movement is often safer than clearing every enemy by force.
priorities: Walk when no immediate threat is chasing the team.
Use bows or knives when a quiet elimination is possible.
Save loud firearms for emergencies or dangerous targets.

steps.title: Collect Treasure and Survival Resources
steps.description: Search the shoreline, jungle structures and abandoned forts for valuables, meat, water and other resources. Fort logbooks can unlock new starting locations for later expeditions.
priorities: Complete the contract's required treasure value first.
Collect optional supplies only while the route remains manageable.
Use treasure-detecting equipment carefully because its loud signal can reveal the crew's position.

steps.title: Adapt Combat to the Weather
steps.description: Flintlock and matchlock weapons depend on dry gunpowder and can fail during rain. Bows reload more quickly than period firearms, while knives remain available when ammunition or weather removes ranged options.
priorities: Check the weather before committing to a firearm attack.
Keep a bow or melee weapon ready when rain begins.
Avoid spending scarce ammunition on distant or unconfirmed shapes.

steps.title: Recognize Escalating Danger
steps.description: Thickening fog, distorted terrain, unnatural colors and a reddening environment signal that the expedition is becoming more dangerous. These changes can also be perceived differently by each teammate.
priorities: Regroup before visibility becomes too poor.
Confirm unusual sights and sounds through voice chat.
Stop chasing optional treasure when the return route is deteriorating.

steps.title: Return Before Greed Wipes the Squad
steps.description: Once the contract is satisfied, decide whether the remaining treasure is worth risking the completed run. Returning safely preserves the haul, advances progression and prepares the crew for a deeper expedition.
priorities: Secure completed objectives before exploring farther.
Retreat when ammunition, visibility or team cohesion becomes poor.
Evaluate the recovered haul aboard the galleon and adjust the next loadout.

bestWeapons.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Best Weapons and Equipment
bestWeapons.intro: No single weapon works in every situation. Quiet bows and knives reduce jungle activity, while firearms provide emergency stopping power at the cost of noise, slow reloads and severe rain weakness.

items.name: Bow
strengths: Does not depend on dry gunpowder.
Reloads faster than period firearms.
Can kill effectively when shots are carefully aimed.
Creates less dangerous noise than a musket.
weaknesses: Slower to fire than an immediate firearm shot.
Requires accurate aim and clear sightlines.
items.role: Primary weapon for the scout or quiet-combat specialist.

items.name: Oil Lamp
items.bestFor: Dark ruins, dense fog and low-visibility routes
strengths: Reveals structures and paths that are difficult to see in darkness.
Helps the group maintain formation in enclosed areas.
weaknesses: Moisture and heavy rain can make the flame flicker.
The lamp carrier becomes an important point of failure if separated.
items.role: Carry at least one lamp and keep a second light source on another explorer when possible.

items.name: Flintlock Musket
strengths: Delivers strong ranged firepower.
Useful when the crew needs an immediate high-impact shot.
weaknesses: Gunpowder can become unusable in rain.
The shot is loud enough to attract additional jungle threats.
items.role: Assign to one disciplined shooter and reserve it for confirmed threats.

items.name: Knife
items.bestFor: Silent finishing attacks and firearm failures
strengths: Works in rain.
Requires no ammunition.
Produces less disturbance than firing a musket.
Remains usable after ranged supplies are exhausted.
weaknesses: Requires the explorer to enter dangerous melee range.
Less suitable when several enemies attack together.
items.role: Every firearm user should have access to a melee fallback.

items.name: Treasure-Finding Medallion
strengths: Guides explorers toward valuable hidden treasure.
Improves the efficiency of loot-focused contracts.
weaknesses: Must be struck to produce its guiding signal.
The resulting metallic clang is loud and can attract danger.
items.role: Use only after the group is ready to defend the area or retreat with the discovered loot.

items.name: Matchlock Musket
strengths: Provides ranged firearm power against physical enemies.
Can be assigned as the crew's dedicated heavy weapon.
weaknesses: Requires careful timing because period firearms reload slowly.
items.role: Bring when the crew expects combat and has separate rain-proof weapons available.

items.name: Balanced Four-Explorer Distribution
strengths: Explorer 1 handles quiet ranged combat with a bow.
Explorer 2 carries a firearm for emergency stopping power.
Explorer 3 manages the oil lamp and navigation.
Explorer 4 operates treasure-finding equipment and supports melee combat.
weaknesses: Roles must be redistributed immediately if an explorer becomes separated or incapacitated.
The team still needs multiple rain-proof backup weapons.
items.role: Keep critical tools spread across the squad rather than concentrating all equipment on one player.

items.name: Abyssal Gear Pack
strengths: Includes Abyssal appearances for the knife and oil lamp.
Includes Abyssal appearances for the flintlock and matchlock muskets.
weaknesses: The pack changes equipment appearance rather than its combat statistics.
items.role: Use for visual customization without changing the crew's functional loadout plan.

sanityGuide.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Sanity and Hallucinations Guide
sanityGuide.intro: The game does not display a conventional sanity meter or warning icon. Instead, each explorer can receive private visual and auditory hallucinations that other teammates do not experience, attacking communication and trust rather than a visible statistic.

cards.title: No Sanity HUD
cards.danger: There is no visible sanity bar, warning siren or interface notification showing when an explorer is losing control.
effects: Illusions appear as normal parts of the environment.
Players may not realize that their perception has changed.
cards.response: Treat sudden environmental changes as information that must be confirmed by another teammate.

cards.title: Bleeding Jungle and Blood Rain
cards.danger: A low-sanity explorer can see the jungle bleeding or experience a localized rain of blood that healthy teammates cannot see.
effects: Terrain colors and environmental details become distorted.
The affected player may believe the entire squad is facing the same event.
cards.response: Ask the nearest teammate whether the visual change is shared before changing direction or using resources.

cards.title: False Horn Calls
cards.danger: Madness can fabricate the familiar horn associated with extraction or a teammate needing help.
effects: Players can be lured away from the group.
Following the sound can lead directly into an ambush.
cards.response: Never follow a distant horn alone. Confirm the cart's location and receive a voice response from the squad first.

cards.title: Teammates Appear as Monsters
cards.danger: Under severe psychological pressure, friendly character models can transform into undead or hostile-looking creatures.
effects: The affected explorer may attack a real teammate.
A single panic shot can turn an unstable fight into a squad disaster.
cards.response: Call out the target's position and wait for voice confirmation before firing at a familiar-sized silhouette.

cards.title: False Teammate Silhouettes
cards.danger: Shapes in fog can resemble familiar explorers even when the real squad is somewhere else.
effects: Players may approach an enemy while believing they have regrouped.
Visual recognition becomes unreliable in low visibility.
cards.response: Use spatial voice chat to compare the direction of the voice with the position of the visible figure.

cards.title: Shifting Terrain and Traps
cards.danger: The environment can appear altered, hiding danger or presenting false routes and tempting objects.
effects: One player may see safe ground where another sees a trap.
Loot-like objects can pull explorers away from the secure path.
cards.response: Let the clearest-minded teammate lead through suspicious terrain and move one explorer at a time.

cards.title: Squad Reality-Check Protocol
cards.danger: Different explorers can experience different versions of the same location.
effects: Simple disagreements can escalate into confusion or friendly fire.
Long explanations waste time during an attack.
cards.response: Use short checks such as 'Do you see it?', 'Did you hear that?' and 'Confirm target ahead' before acting.

cards.title: Stay Within Communication Range
cards.danger: Spatial voice chat becomes quieter and changes direction as teammates move farther apart.
effects: Separated explorers lose their most reliable way to test reality.
Hallucinated voices become harder to distinguish from real calls.
cards.response: Maintain visual or voice contact and regroup immediately when fog, combat or panic separates the formation.

cards.title: Handling a Fully Fractured Explorer
cards.danger: An explorer experiencing intense madness may no longer be able to trust any sight or sound.
effects: The player can waste ammunition or lead the squad away from extraction.
cards.response: The affected explorer should stop making navigation and target decisions, follow direct squad instructions and remain close to a confirmed teammate.

coopCrossplay.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Co-op, Solo and Crossplay Guide
coopCrossplay.intro: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu supports solo expeditions and online squads of two to four explorers. Communication and clearly divided responsibilities become increasingly important as madness distorts voices, silhouettes and team awareness.

items.support: A complete single-player mode is available.
items.use: Carry a balanced loadout that covers navigation, light, ranged combat and close-range defense because there are no teammates to protect the cart or confirm hallucinations.

items.support: Online co-op supports two to four players.
items.use: Use smaller squads for careful resource management or a full four-player party to divide navigation, combat, gathering and cart protection.

items.support: The Epic Games Store lists cross-platform play. Xbox supports cross-platform co-op across Xbox Series X|S, Windows PC and Xbox Cloud Gaming through Xbox Play Anywhere.
items.use: Add the group before starting an expedition and confirm that every player has joined the same session before selecting a contract.

items.support: Voices are positioned according to each explorer's location in the environment.
items.use: Keep callouts short and repeat important directions. A distant or strangely positioned voice may indicate that the speaker is separated or that perception is being distorted.

items.support: One player tracks the route, abandoned forts, logbooks and the path back to extraction.
items.use: Stay near the front without moving beyond voice range. Logbooks recovered from forts can unlock new starting points for later expeditions.

items.support: The ox cart carries the treasure required by the captain's contract.
items.use: Remain close to the cart during attacks, watch the rear of the group and warn the team before the route becomes blocked.

items.support: Remaining players can divide ranged combat, close defense and the collection of treasure, relics, meat, water and other resources.
items.use: Avoid having every player search the same ruin. Keep at least one armed explorer covering the group while loot is moved to the cart.

items.support: Madness can create familiar silhouettes and apparent teammates that are not part of the real squad.
items.use: Confirm identity through spatial voice chat and a simple agreed call-and-response before following or firing at a figure in heavy fog.

contractsExtraction.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Contracts, Treasure and Extraction Guide
contractsExtraction.intro: Every expedition begins with a captain's contract and ends with a decision: protect the required treasure or risk the entire haul by pushing deeper into the jungle of The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu.

steps.title: Choose the Captain's Contract
steps.objective: Select an expedition and review the treasure requirement aboard the galleon.
steps.action: Treat the requested treasure value as the primary objective. The captain provides gear in exchange for the valuables brought back by the crew.
steps.risk: Choose a route and loadout that match the squad size instead of selecting equipment only for maximum firepower.

steps.title: Divide Weapons and Tools
steps.objective: Give every explorer a clear responsibility before departure.
steps.action: Split ranged weapons, close-combat equipment, lamps and navigation tools across the party. Keep one player ready to defend the cart while others search nearby structures.
steps.risk: Do not place every ranged weapon or essential tool in one player's inventory.

steps.title: Escort the Ox Cart
steps.objective: Move the expedition's treasure carrier through the jungle.
steps.action: The cart follows the group and stores valuables collected during the expedition. Keep the route wide enough for the cart and regroup before entering ruins or dense vegetation.
steps.risk: Avoid leaving the cart isolated while the whole squad searches in different directions.

steps.title: Secure the Required Treasure First
steps.objective: Reach the value requested by the captain before hunting optional rewards.
steps.action: Place recovered valuables directly into the cart and track whether the contract requirement has been covered.
steps.risk: Once the required haul is secured, treat every additional detour as an optional risk rather than part of the main objective.

steps.title: Collect Survival Resources
steps.objective: Bring back useful materials in addition to treasure.
steps.action: Search for relics, cursed treasures, meat, water and other resources. Hunted meat can be processed aboard the ship, while special items such as the medallion can help locate valuable Tulu-metal treasure.
steps.risk: Treasure-finding tools can create loud sounds, attracting threats and increasing pressure on the squad.

steps.title: Control Noise and Escalation
steps.objective: Prevent the jungle from becoming fully active before extraction.
steps.action: Limit unnecessary running, shouting, gunfire and vegetation cutting. Watch for thicker fog, red growth, changing light and increasing undead activity.
steps.risk: Rain can make gunpowder weapons unusable, so keep bows or close-range weapons available instead of relying entirely on muskets.

steps.title: Extract or Push Deeper
steps.objective: Decide whether the next reward is worth risking the completed contract.
steps.action: Extract when the required treasure is secured, resources are low, the squad is separated or the jungle has entered a dangerous state. Continue only when the group remains equipped, organized and able to protect the cart.
steps.risk: Abandoned forts and their logbooks provide lasting progression by unlocking new expedition starting points, making them valuable targets when the route remains manageable.

monstersBestiary.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu Monsters and Bestiary
monstersBestiary.intro: Combat in The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu is deliberately slow and uncertain. Players must read enemy behavior, preserve ammunition and distinguish real threats from hallucinations before reacting.

cards.name: Y'm-bhi Sentries
cards.behavior: Y'm-bhi watch players, test their distance and build stress instead of always attacking immediately. They may retreat into the forest and reposition for an ambush.
cards.warning: A watching corpse, fragments of human speech and glowing eyes revealed through a crucifix indicate rising stress.
cards.counter: Use a crucifix to read their stress, maintain distance and use light to blind them briefly in darkness. Light is less effective when the Y'm-bhi carries its own lamp.
cards.rule: Some lamp-carrying Y'm-bhi can set themselves on fire and charge. Killing one may not be permanent because the forest can reanimate its body.

cards.name: Undead Wanderers
cards.behavior: Undead enemies shamble through beaches, ruins, fog and jungle routes, often appearing in greater numbers as the expedition creates noise.
cards.warning: Movement inside fog, silhouettes near ruins and increasing enemy numbers after running, shooting or cutting through vegetation.
cards.counter: Use ranged weapons before they close the distance, then switch to a knife or create space while slow firearms reload. Undead bodies can provide weapons or ammunition.

cards.name: Corrupted Explorers
cards.type: Fallen player transformed into an enemy
cards.behavior: An explorer who dies and is not revived promptly can return as a corrupted undead hostile that hunts the surviving squad.
cards.warning: A downed teammate left unattended while the remaining party continues moving.
cards.counter: Prioritize safe revives, protect the rescuer and confirm that the recovered explorer has rejoined the group before continuing.

cards.name: False Teammates
cards.behavior: Madness can produce familiar human silhouettes that appear to be members of the squad even when the real players are elsewhere.
cards.warning: A teammate appears unexpectedly through dense fog, does not answer correctly or conflicts with the position indicated by spatial voice chat.
cards.counter: Use an agreed voice response, regroup at a visible landmark and avoid firing or following until identity is confirmed.

cards.name: Spectres and Apparitions
cards.behavior: Spectral figures and unexplained phenomena accompany the cart and expedition as the boundary between reality and illusion breaks down.
cards.warning: Sudden figures, distorted sounds, changing surroundings and threats that appear without a clear physical approach.
cards.counter: Stay with the cart, rely on squad communication and avoid wasting ammunition on an image that has not shown consistent physical behavior.

cards.name: The Living Jungle
cards.behavior: The island reacts to noise and prolonged exploration. Fog thickens, visibility closes, roots and ground change color, and hostile activity escalates.
cards.warning: A red moon, pulsing red vegetation, rapidly thickening mist and more frequent enemies around the squad.
cards.counter: Reduce noise, stop optional looting, consolidate the party around the cart and begin extraction before perception becomes unreliable.

systemRequirements.title: The Mound Omen of Cthulhu System Requirements and Performance
systemRequirements.intro: The PC version of The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu requires 16 GB of RAM and DirectX 12 at both specification levels. NVIDIA RTX 50 Series systems can use DLSS 4.5 for higher frame rates and clearer jungle imagery.

items.priority: Use a fully updated 64-bit installation with current graphics drivers.

items.minimum: Intel Core i7-8700K or AMD Ryzen 5 1600X
items.recommended: Intel Core i7-11700K or AMD Ryzen 5 5500
items.priority: The recommended processor provides more headroom during combat, co-op sessions and dense environmental scenes.
Close heavy background applications before launching the game to preserve available memory.

items.minimum: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER 6 GB, AMD Radeon RX 5600 XT 6 GB or Intel Arc A770 16 GB
items.recommended: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 8 GB or AMD Radeon RX 6750 XT 12 GB
items.priority: The recommended GPUs are better suited to maintaining visual clarity while rendering fog, foliage, lighting and multiple enemies.

items.minimum: Not required for the base specification
items.recommended: Supported at launch for GeForce RTX 50 Series GPUs with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and Super Resolution using the second-generation transformer mode
items.priority: Enable Super Resolution first, then use frame generation when additional smoothness is needed.

items.minimum: Lower shadows, foliage density and volumetric effects first when frame rate drops
items.recommended: Keep texture quality and view distance as high as available graphics memory allows
items.priority: Stable movement and clear silhouettes are more useful than maximum fog, shadow and foliage detail during expeditions.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu

questions.question: Is The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu single-player or co-op?
questions.answer: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu supports both. You can play solo or form an online co-op squad of two to four explorers, with cross-platform features available through supported platforms.

questions.question: What platforms is The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu available on?
questions.answer: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu is available on PC (Steam, Epic Games Store and Xbox PC Store), PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. There is no PlayStation 4, Xbox One or Nintendo Switch version.

questions.question: When does The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu release and how much does it cost?
questions.answer: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu launched on July 15, 2026. The Standard Edition is $29.99 and the Deluxe Edition is $39.99, with a 20% Steam launch discount that unlocked after the game passed 800,000 wishlists.

questions.question: Does The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu support crossplay?
questions.answer: Yes. The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu lists cross-platform play, and the Xbox version supports cross-platform co-op across Xbox Series X|S, Windows PC and Xbox Cloud Gaming through Xbox Play Anywhere.

questions.question: How does sanity work in The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu?
questions.answer: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu has no visible sanity meter. Instead, explorers receive private hallucinations that distort terrain, sounds and even the appearance of teammates, so squads must verify threats through spatial voice chat before acting.

questions.question: What are the PC system requirements for The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu?
questions.answer: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu requires 16 GB of RAM and DirectX 12 on a 64-bit Windows 10 or 11 system. NVIDIA RTX 50 Series GPUs can also use DLSS 4.5 for smoother performance in dense jungle environments.
